title: Topology — federated canvas-based organisational modelling (MVP)
scope:
Build Vector's Topology surface — a first-class canvas-based product page, named per tenant (e.g. 'MMFFDev: Topology'), where gadmin (and a single delegated padmin per node) draws the organisation as a free-form tree of block diagrams. The default noun for a node is Office, overrideable per node. The canvas is the source of truth for the org model; nothing else is. The org tree powers Workspace scoping (Path C clarified per R028 §8 — container is itself a tree), per-node clamp policy, rollups, audit boundaries, and cross-team move semantics across the rest of Vector.

This plan supersedes the original Path C primitive in R028. Where R028 said "Workspace as a clamp predicate at view time", PLA-0006 makes the container an editable tree (C.2): an org_nodes table with self-referential parent_id, drawn on a Vector-built diagram primitive (Canvas2D + dagre + d3-zoom) with collapse-by-default for enterprise scale (target: 3,000-node Lloyds-class trees rendering in <1.5s and sustaining 30 FPS during drag, with rendered set capped at ~500 via collapse + virtualisation per R029 findings).

Build vs buy: build. R029's library evaluation showed React Flow has known performance cliffs at our target scale, GoJS is commercial-only with a licence that forbids derivative works (year-one cost £15k–£40k for a SaaS team — same as the engineering effort to build it), and Cytoscape.js doesn't host arbitrary HTML in nodes. We need ~5% of what a general-purpose graph library provides — most of GoJS is bloat for our use case. Owning the substrate means we ship the exact behaviour we want, the licence is ours, and the primitive becomes part of the Samantha API surface (samantha.diagram.canvas) so anyone building a custom app or custom page can render their own diagrams. Estimated build: 3–5 weeks for one senior engineer using dagre (MIT) for layout and d3-zoom (BSD) for pan/zoom transforms over a Canvas2D renderer.

Federated authoring via delegated handoff (single-level in MVP). Gadmin draws the top-level skeleton and assigns one padmin per top-level Office. Each padmin lands on the canvas clamped to their subtree and continues modelling from there. Padmin re-delegation to leads is deferred to Phase X — schema ships with can_redelegate so we don't migrate later, but the UI doesn't expose it. The whole top-level contributes to the model; the gadmin is not a bottleneck. None of Rally / Jira / Azure DevOps / GitLab does this — it's a product differentiator.

Permission model. Subscription roles (gadmin / padmin / user) remain for cross-cutting concerns (billing, login). Inside Topology, roles are node-scoped via a new org_node_roles table. A user can hold different roles on different nodes (admin on one Office, viewer on another). The clamp predicate and the role predicate share the same recursive-CTE machinery. MVP enforces at most one active admin grant per node; multi-admin is deferred to Phase X.

Archive = limbo, not delete. Archiving a node (and its entire subtree) greys it out on the canvas, preserves all role grants and FK relationships, and excludes the subtree from default queries unless the caller asks for archived. Cascade semantics for the deep-worm-hole case (a node with children getting archived, then individually un-archived) are deferred to Phase X.

What this plan does NOT do (Phase X deferrals). No padmin re-delegation in UI. No multi-admin per node. No archive-cascade UX. No matrix orgs (a node has exactly one parent in MVP). No org-versioning / what-if branching. No EA-grade artefact features (capability maps, application portfolios, dependency views) — schema supports the upgrade path but UX stays focused on functional MVP. No third-party canvas library — the diagram primitive is Vector's.
value:
• Lloyds-scale modelling becomes possible. 1,000–3,000 nodes drawable, navigable, and editable by the people who actually know each branch — not by one gadmin guessing.
  • Eliminates the Rally "Project" naming and UX failure (20-minute trainings spent defending the noun) by naming the page neutrally and letting tenants name their own levels.
  • Workspace scoping (Path C) gets a real home. Every other clamp / rollup / audit feature in Vector reads from this one tree — no parallel partition scheme to maintain.
  • Cross-team moves become honest. Drag a Team between divisions on the canvas; system shows a preview diff (work items reparented, dashboards shifted, access changes) before commit.
  • Federated authoring is a unique market position. No work-management or EA tool ships delegated org-modelling. This is the "oh — finally" moment for transformation-era enterprises.
  • Diagram primitive becomes a Samantha API capability. Once we own the canvas, custom-app and custom-page authors can render their own diagrams (process flows, dependency maps, customer journeys, capability overlays) using the same primitive Org Design uses. The investment pays back across every future visualisation feature.
  • No third-party licence drag. No annual GoJS bill, no React Flow scale-cliff to manage around, no vendor lock-in. The primitive is ours; the bloat we don't need (UML, Sankey, swim-lanes, BPMN templates) we don't carry.
  • Schema supports upgrade to EA-grade artefact later (capability/application/data overlays) without migration; UX investment is incremental.
implementation_plan: MVP cut (locked 2026-05-02): page name is '<tenant>: Topology' (heading + browser title); default node noun is 'Office'; delegation is single-level (gadmin → padmin only) — `can_redelegate` ships in schema but UI doesn't expose it; at most one active admin grant per node — schema unique constraint enforces it; archive = limbo (greyed-out subtree, role grants + FKs preserved, default queries hide unless asked for archived). Padmin re-delegation, multi-admin, archive-cascade, matrix orgs, org-versioning, EA overlays = Phase X.

Migration A: org_nodes table — UUID PK, parent_id self-FK (nullable, root has NULL), subscription_id FK, name, description, label_override (gadmin-named level e.g. 'Department' / 'Squad'), icon, colour, avatar_url, layout_mode enum ('auto-horizontal' | 'auto-vertical' | 'auto-radial' | 'manual'), manual_x / manual_y (nullable, only when layout_mode='manual'), collapsed_default bool, position int (sort among siblings), soft_archive, audit columns. Unique (subscription_id, parent_id, name). Recursive-CTE-friendly index on (subscription_id, parent_id).

Migration B: org_node_roles table — UUID PK, node_id FK, user_id FK, role enum ('admin' | 'editor' | 'viewer'), can_redelegate bool default false (schema-ready for Phase X; UI does not expose in MVP), granted_by FK users, granted_at, revoked_at nullable. Unique (node_id, user_id) where revoked_at IS NULL. Additional MVP constraint: unique (node_id, role='admin') where revoked_at IS NULL — enforces single-admin-per-node; relax in Phase X.

Migration C: org_node_view_state per-user — node_id FK, user_id FK, collapsed bool, last_viewed_at. Unique (node_id, user_id). Keeps canvas expand/collapse state per-user without polluting shared layout.

Migration D: add org_node_id FK column to portfolio_items (nullable for now, NOT NULL after backfill of root-node fallback per subscription). Same on user_stories.org_node_id. Indexes on (org_node_id) for clamp predicate.

Backend: orgdesign.Service Go module — sole writer for org_nodes, org_node_roles, org_node_view_state. Methods: CreateNode, RenameNode, MoveNode (parent change with cycle check), ArchiveNode, GrantRole, RevokeRole, SetViewState, BulkPosition (debounced canvas writes), Subtree (recursive-CTE walk), AncestorsOf, ClampPredicate(user_id) → subtree of user's lowest admin/editor/viewer node.

Backend: REST surface — POST /api/topology/nodes (create), PATCH /api/topology/nodes/:id (rename/move/colour/icon), DELETE /api/topology/nodes/:id (soft-archive), POST /api/topology/nodes/bulk-position (canvas drag commit), GET /api/topology/tree?root=:id&depth=N (lazy-expand fetch), POST /api/topology/nodes/:id/roles (grant), DELETE /api/topology/nodes/:id/roles/:userId (revoke), GET /api/topology/preview-move?node=:id&newParent=:pid (work-items / dashboards / access diff before commit).

Backend: clamp predicate hook — every list-style read endpoint that touches portfolio_items or user_stories accepts an implicit clamp via ClampPredicate(currentUser); flagged as a cross-cutting middleware so feature teams don't re-implement it.

Frontend primitive: Vector Diagram Canvas — new package app/components/diagram-canvas/ exposing a generic <DiagramCanvas> React component. Renderer is Canvas2D (single <canvas> element) with off-screen layer for static elements + on-screen layer for hover/drag affordances. Layout is dagre (MIT) running in a Web Worker. Pan/zoom is d3-zoom (BSD). Provides: node hit-test, edge routing (orthogonal + bezier), drag-and-drop with cycle prevention hooks, minimap, fitView, viewport state, debounced position commits. Pluggable node renderer (consumer supplies a render function for any node shape). Pluggable edge style. No third-party graph library.

Frontend primitive: stress harness — synthetic 3,000-node tree fixture lives in dev/tests/diagram-canvas-stress.spec.ts; gates the primitive itself (independent of Topology page); load <1.5s, 30 FPS drag, <500 rendered set after collapse-by-default.

Frontend: /topology page — full-bleed <DiagramCanvas>, MiniMap top-right, Controls (zoom/fit/lock) bottom-right, dotted background, breadcrumbs along top, search bar top-left (server-side fuzzy → fitView({ nodeId: matchId })). Loads root + 2 levels by default; lazy-fetches subtrees on expand. OrgNode renderer = block diagram with name, icon, colour bar, member-count badge, child-count badge.

Frontend: collapse-by-default + lazy expand — every node renders with children hidden until clicked; fetches subtree on expand; rendered-set cap enforced by data-layer filtering inside <DiagramCanvas>.

Frontend: drag-and-drop interaction — drop on existing node = make child; drop between siblings = reorder. onNodeDragStop fires debounced bulk-position write. Cycle-prevention enforced both client-side (<DiagramCanvas> rejects drop targets that would loop) and server-side (orgdesign.Service rejects). Detach-to-root via canvas drop is gadmin-only and deferred to Phase X — MVP allows reparent only between existing nodes.

Frontend: side panel on selection — name, description, label_override (default 'Office'), icon, colour, layout_mode, members list, single admin grant (one active admin per node in MVP), clamp_policy (inherit / open / restrict-subtree / restrict-node), archive button (places node and subtree into limbo — greyed). MVP UI does NOT expose can_redelegate or multi-admin (Phase X).

Frontend: cross-team move preview modal — fires on drag-commit when target subtree differs from source subtree; shows diff (N work items reparented, M dashboards shift, K users lose access); confirm or cancel before write.

Frontend: handoff inbox — when a user is granted admin/editor on a node, they get a notification (existing notifications system) with a deep-link to /topology?focus=:nodeId; landing on it zooms the canvas to their node and presents an empty-state CTA "Define this <label_override>'s structure".

Frontend: empty state — first gadmin lands on a single root block labelled with the subscription's display name (e.g. 'MMFFDev'); CTA 'Add your first Office'. No fixed taxonomy, no wizard. Browser title and page heading both show '<tenant-name>: Topology'.

Samantha API exposure: <DiagramCanvas> is registered as a Samantha-callable primitive (samantha.diagram.canvas) — custom-app and custom-page authors can mount it from their app manifest, supply a node-renderer callback, and bind to their own data source. Documented in the Samantha SDK reference; example app shipped ("My team's process flow") to prove the contract.
Frontend: list view fallback — /topology?view=list reads same data, supports same operations (rename / move / grant role / archive); accessible to screen readers; canvas remains primary surface.

Frontend: address-substrate adoption — every panel on /topology wraps in <Panel> per PLA-0005; canvas itself registers as samantha._viewport.app._kind.topology.canvas. <DiagramCanvas> primitive accepts an addressableId prop so any embed (Topology, custom apps) gets a unique address.

Docs — docs/c_c_org_design.md leaf doc covering schema, clamp predicate machinery, role model, handoff flow, naming open-questions; docs/c_c_diagram_canvas.md covering the primitive's API, performance contract, Samantha SDK exposure; CLAUDE.md pointers; c_scope.md Underway row; R028 §8 cross-link; R029 cross-link as the build-vs-buy evidence base.

Frontend primitive: snap-to-grid — <DiagramCanvas> renders a dotted background grid (default spacing 10px; configurable via gridSize prop). All node positions during drag and on commit snap to the nearest grid intersection. Auto-layout (dagre) results are rounded to the grid before render. Grid is visually subtle (low-opacity dots) and toggleable per-page via a `showGrid` prop (default true on /topology). Gates manual layout_mode behaviour — manual_x / manual_y values are persisted as multiples of gridSize.
areas_impacted: SQL: four migrations (org_nodes, org_node_roles, org_node_view_state, FK columns on portfolio_items + user_stories with backfill).
API: new orgdesign.Service Go module (sole writer); ~10 new REST endpoints; cross-cutting clamp predicate middleware on every list endpoint touching portfolio_items / user_stories.

UI: new app/components/diagram-canvas/ primitive (Canvas2D + dagre worker + d3-zoom — no third-party graph library); new /topology page consuming it; new OrgNode renderer; side panel; preview-move modal; empty state; list-view fallback; handoff notification integration.
API (Samantha SDK): new samantha.diagram.canvas surface — custom-app and custom-page authors can mount <DiagramCanvas>; documented; reference example app shipped.
DEV: stress fixture (3,000-node Lloyds tree) gates both the primitive and the Topology page; acceptance test gate; lint:addressables coverage on new page.
GOV: handoff flow lands as a first-class governance feature — node-scoped admin grants are auditable and revocable.

SEC: trust-no-one boundary at orgdesign.Service — direct INSERTs to org_nodes / org_node_roles outside the service forbidden; clamp predicate is server-enforced (UI clamp is convenience only); cycle-check server-side; preview-move requires re-confirmation server-side at commit.
AUD: every node mutation logged (created / renamed / moved / archived / role-granted / role-revoked) with actor + timestamp; queryable via existing audit reports.
DOC: two new leaf docs (org_design + diagram_canvas) + index pointers + scope row + R028/R029 cross-references.
feature_list: org_nodes table — self-referential tree, gadmin-named levels, layout metadata, soft-archive.
org_node_roles table — node-scoped role grants with re-delegation flag.
FK columns on portfolio_items + user_stories pointing to org_nodes.
orgdesign.Service Go module — sole writer; recursive-CTE subtree walks; cycle check; clamp predicate.
REST surface — node CRUD, bulk position, lazy-expand subtree fetch, role grants, preview-move, search.
Cross-cutting clamp predicate middleware — every relevant list endpoint reads it.
<DiagramCanvas> primitive — Canvas2D + dagre worker + d3-zoom; pluggable node renderer; stress-tested at 3,000 nodes; no third-party graph library.
Samantha API exposure — samantha.diagram.canvas surface for custom-app / custom-page authors; reference example app.
/topology canvas page — consumes <DiagramCanvas>; MiniMap, Controls, breadcrumbs, search, side panel, preview-move modal.
Collapse-by-default + lazy expand — keeps rendered-set under ~500 even at 3,000-node scale.
Drag-and-drop reparent with cycle prevention and cross-team move preview.
Handoff inbox — notification + deep-link when granted node-scoped admin/editor.
Stress acceptance — 3,000-node Lloyds fixture, <1.5s load, 30 FPS drag, <1s subtree layout (gates the primitive).
List view fallback at /topology?view=list — accessibility + power-user surface.
Snap-to-grid in <DiagramCanvas> — 10px dotted grid, drag/drop and auto-layout snap to grid intersections.
features_extended: R028 §8 extends: Path C is now C.2 — Workspace container is itself a tree, not a flat tag; this plan delivers that schema and surface.
PLA-0005 extends: Org Design page adopts the addressables substrate from day one; every panel is <Panel>-wrapped; canvas registers an address.

Subscription role model extends: Existing gadmin / padmin / user roles remain at subscription level; org_node_roles introduces node-scoped admin/editor/viewer that overlays them within Org Design and clamp scope.

Samantha API extends: New samantha.diagram.canvas primitive becomes a reusable visualisation capability across the Samantha SDK — custom apps and custom pages can render their own diagrams without taking a third-party dependency.

Canvas management UX (added 2026-05-03): stories 00310–00322 add the daily-driver authoring surface — animated edit flyout, write-through field persistence, +/- spawn buttons on every node side, span-wide level dividers, left-edge level-label boxes, mini parent/child tree-icon view, disconnect-not-delete semantics, disconnected-nodes tray, gadmin commit flag with banner-on-dirty re-commit. Schema gains org_levels (first-class level entities seeded with Organisation/Department/Division), org_nodes.description, org_nodes.level_id, subscriptions.topology_committed_at/_by.
features_removed: Implicit assumption that 'Workspace' is a flat tag (R028 v1) — superseded; Workspace is now a tree node.
Single-author assumption (only gadmin can model the org) — superseded by federated handoff via org_node_roles.

Third-party canvas library dependency (React Flow / GoJS / Cytoscape.js) — superseded by Vector-built <DiagramCanvas> primitive over Canvas2D + dagre + d3-zoom; the primitive becomes a Samantha API surface.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Schema in place: org_nodes (self-referential tree), org_node_roles (node-scoped grants with re-delegate flag), org_node_view_state (per-user collapse), and org_node_id FK columns on portfolio_items + user_stories — all migrated and backfilled, no orphaned work items.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Migration runs cleanly on dev/staging/prod; backfill query reports zero null org_node_id rows; goose status reports up-to-date.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: orgdesign.Service is the sole writer for org_nodes, org_node_roles, org_node_view_state — direct INSERTs from outside the service are blocked at the lint/CI level (per the trust-no-one pattern from PLA-0005).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: lint:writer-boundary passes; grep for 'INSERT INTO org_nodes' outside backend/orgdesign returns zero matches.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <DiagramCanvas> primitive renders a 3,000-node synthetic Lloyds tree in <1.5s, sustains 30 FPS during drag, and keeps the rendered-node set under 500 via collapse-by-default + lazy expand. Stress test gates the primitive independently of the Org Design page.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Stress fixture test in dev/tests/diagram-canvas-stress.spec.ts passes in CI; performance metrics logged in test output; primitive runs without org-design code mounted.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <DiagramCanvas> is exposed via Samantha API (samantha.diagram.canvas) and the reference example app ("My team's process flow" or equivalent) renders successfully from a custom app manifest with a custom node renderer and a custom data source.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Reference app loads in /apps/<example-id>; custom node renderer fires; pan/zoom/drag all work; address devtool can hover the embedded canvas and copy a valid address.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Federated handoff works end-to-end: gadmin creates a node and grants admin role to user X; user X receives a notification with deep-link; user X lands on /topology clamped to their subtree; user X can edit within the subtree but not outside it; if can_redelegate=true, user X can grant admin to user Y on a descendant node.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: End-to-end test in dev/tests/topology-handoff.spec.ts; manual gadmin → padmin → lead chain verified on staging.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Clamp predicate is enforced server-side on every list endpoint that touches portfolio_items or user_stories — UI clamp is convenience only.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Penetration test: a viewer with clamp anchor on Engineering attempts to read Compliance items via direct API call; server returns 403 / empty; logged as denied.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Cross-team move preview shows the correct diff before commit: count of work items reparented, dashboards affected, users gaining/losing access. Cancel discards; confirm commits.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Integration test with seeded fixture: move a Team from Engineering to Back Office; preview shows expected counts; commit applies; cancel leaves state untouched.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Cycle prevention works on both client (drop target rejected if it'd loop) and server (commit rejected with cycle error if client check is bypassed).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Test: attempt to drop Engineering onto its own descendant; client UI rejects with toast; direct API call returns 422 cycle-error.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: List view fallback at /topology?view=list reads the same data, supports the same operations (rename, move, grant role, archive), and is screen-reader accessible.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Axe accessibility audit passes on /topology?view=list; functional test suite covers all canvas operations through the list surface.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every panel on /topology adopts <Panel> per PLA-0005; canvas registers samantha._viewport.app._kind.topology.canvas; lint:addressables passes.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Address devtool can hover the canvas and copy a valid address; lint:addressables on /topology returns zero violations.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: MVP naming + scope decisions are honoured: page is named '<tenant>: Topology' (e.g. 'MMFFDev: Topology'); default node noun is 'Office' (overrideable per node); delegation is single-level (gadmin → padmin only — re-delegation deferred to Phase X); at most one active admin grant per node (multi-admin deferred to Phase X); archive places the node and its subtree in greyed-out limbo with role grants and FKs preserved (cascade semantics deferred to Phase X).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: UI copy uses 'Topology' and 'Office'; e2e test verifies single-level delegation gate; e2e test verifies multi-admin is rejected; archive test verifies subtree is greyed but kept; docs/c_c_org_design.md decisions table matches.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <DiagramCanvas> renders a dotted background grid at 10px spacing by default; node drag commits snap to the nearest grid intersection; auto-layout outputs are rounded to the grid; the grid is toggleable via the showGrid prop and visible by default on /topology.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit test verifies position rounding to gridSize on drop; visual test confirms dotted grid renders and aligns to manual_x/manual_y values; toggle test verifies showGrid=false hides the grid; default page load on /topology shows grid on.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A "Reset canvas" gadmin-only button on /topology opens a confirm dialog requiring the tenant name to be typed before the action commits.
POST /api/orgdesign/reset soft-archives every non-root org_node for the subscription and re-seeds the root row idempotently; non-gadmin callers receive 403.
Every reset emits a topology.canvas.reset audit entry naming the actor, IP, and the count of nodes archived.
After reset, refreshing /topology lands on the empty-state CTA — no canvas-rendered nodes other than root.
Clicking a node animates the canvas viewport so the node sits in the visible-area centre (account for flyout width) over ~300ms easing-out, never instant-jumping.
The flyout mounts as a right-side overlay panel that slides in from x=100% to its docked width with the same ~300ms easing; ESC and click-outside slide it back out and cancel the centring.
The flyout registers as `<Panel name="topology_edit_flyout">` so Samantha resolves it via samantha._viewport.app._kind.panel.topology_edit_flyout.
Focus traps inside the flyout while open; pressing ESC returns focus to the previously-selected node.
New migration adds org_nodes.description text NOT NULL DEFAULT '' and the orgdesign service exposes PatchDescription.
Editing name, description, or label_override fires a single PATCH per field within 250ms of last keystroke; reload renders the saved values without further input.
Each field edit emits a topology.node.renamed / topology.node.described / topology.node.relabelled audit row.
Killing the tab mid-edit and reopening /topology shows the most-recent saved state — no client buffer is consulted.

New migration creates org_levels (id uuid PK, subscription_id uuid FK, depth int, name text, position int, audit cols, UNIQUE (subscription_id, depth)) and adds org_nodes.level_id uuid NOT NULL FK with backfill from existing tree depth.

orgdesign.Service gains CreateLevel, RenameLevel, ListLevels, and an internal resolveLevelForDepth helper that auto-creates a level row when a node spawns at a depth with no existing level; sole-writer boundary test extended to cover org_levels.
Service-layer check rejects any CreateNode / MoveNode whose resolved tree depth differs from the requested level_id's depth (sentinel ErrLevelDepthMismatch → 400).
Unit test covers (a) auto-create on first occupant of a new depth, (b) idempotent re-create returns existing row, (c) cross-tenant level isolation.

Seed migration inserts three org_levels rows (depths 0/1/2, names "Organisation"/"Department"/"Division") for every existing subscription idempotently and for every new subscription via the provision-on-first-gadmin trigger.
GET /api/orgdesign/levels returns three rows for any tenant on first load with the correct names and depths.
Spawning a node at depth-3 (no level row yet) creates an org_levels row for depth-3 named "Level 4" and the node's level_id resolves to it.
PATCH /api/orgdesign/levels/:id with {name: "Region"} updates the row; canvas re-renders the row band label without page reload.
DiagramCanvas renderNode draws +/- glyphs on all four sides of every non-root node and only below for root; hit zones are 16x16px and live above the node body.

Clicking + on any side fires CreateNode (or MoveNode for parent-up) via topologyApi; the new node appears at the standard sibling padding and snap-grid position; level_id resolves to the appropriate org_levels row (auto-creating one if needed).
Clicking - fires the disconnect path (parent_id → null) via PatchNode; the node remains in org_nodes and is reachable via the disconnected-nodes tray (story 00321).
Every spawn / disconnect via these buttons emits a topology.node.created or topology.node.disconnected audit row naming the actor, the affected node, and the directional intent.

A new app/components/diagram-canvas/geometry.ts exports NODE_W=140, NODE_H=56, COL_GAP=40, ROW_GAP=80, GRID=10 as the single source of truth; both buildDiagramFixture and the spawn handler import from it.
A + on any side places the new node at exactly the prevailing padding and at a Y matching existing siblings in the same row band; coordinates are multiples of GRID.
Every node rendered on the canvas has the exact same width and height; no element renders with a custom size (visual regression test asserts equality across a 5-node fixture).
onNodeDragStop snaps to GRID and re-clamps Y to the level row band; cross-band drags trigger the move-preview modal not a free-form Y change.
The canvas renders one dashed-dotted line per gap between org_levels depths; the line spans the full visible canvas width and pans/zooms with the world transform.
Adding a new org_levels row (auto-create on deeper spawn) immediately renders an additional divider; deleting/archiving a level row removes its divider.
The divider stroke matches the connector stroke (same dash pattern, same colour, same width) — driven by a single token in geometry.ts / theme.
At any zoom level [0.25x..2.0x], dividers render without artefacts and remain horizontal.
A column of <Panel name="topology_level_label_<depth>"> boxes pins to the canvas left edge with identical W=120, H=80 (constants in geometry.ts); never staggered; one box per org_levels row.
Clicking the box reveals an inline input bound to org_levels.name; blur or Enter writes through via PATCH /api/orgdesign/levels/:id; ESC reverts to the persisted value.
Non-gadmin actors see the box read-only; PATCH on the endpoint returns 403 for non-gadmin.
Every level rename emits a topology.level.renamed audit row with old_name and new_name fields.
The flyout's right column shows up to 3 generations centred on the selected node (parent + selected + children); rendering uses ASCII-style elbow and T glyphs in a fixed-width font.
Clicking any name in the tree-icon view fires onNodeSelect, animates the flyout (story 00311) onto the new node, and scrolls the tree-icon view to keep the new selection centred.
The tree-icon view re-renders when org_nodes for the selected branch change (parent rename, child added via +, child disconnected via -).
The view registers as <Panel name="topology_tree_icon"> so Samantha can target it via the addressables substrate.

orgdesign.Service.DisconnectNode(ctx, sub, nodeID, actor) sets parent_id=NULL on the target node, leaves all descendants in place (their parent_id chain still resolves to the now-orphaned subtree root), and emits a single audit row.
No DELETE statement runs; SELECT count(*) FROM org_nodes is unchanged before and after a disconnect.
A subsequent topologyApi.move(disconnectedNodeID, newParentID) reattaches the entire subtree under the new parent; depth/level_id auto-resolves.

The canvas removes the disconnected subtree from the main render and adds it to the disconnected tray; clicking - on the root row is rejected with a sentinel error (root cannot disconnect from itself).
New GET /api/orgdesign/disconnected returns an array of {id, name, descendant_count, disconnected_at, disconnected_by}; respects clamp predicate.
A new <Panel name="topology_disconnected_tray"> renders the list at the bottom of /topology; collapsible; row count badge in the title.
Clicking "Reconnect…" on a row opens the existing PreviewMoveModal with the orphan as the source and a parent picker; commit reattaches the subtree and removes the row from the tray.
Tray and every reconnect button register as addressables under samantha._viewport.app._kind.panel.topology_disconnected_tray.
New migration adds subscriptions.topology_committed_at timestamptz NULL and subscriptions.topology_committed_by uuid NULL FK users.
orgdesign.Service.Commit(ctx, sub, actor) sets the two columns; service rejects non-gadmin (sentinel ErrCommitForbidden → 403). Re-commit re-stamps both fields.
When any topology mutation lands with topology_committed_at NOT NULL and the mutation post-dates committed_at, /topology renders a dirty-since-commit banner with a "Re-commit" button (gadmin only).
topology.committed and topology.recommitted audit rows include actor, IP, descendant_count, and the previous committed_at.

risks.risk: Build-our-own-canvas effort is under-scoped — Vector engineers spend longer than the 3–5 week estimate on the <DiagramCanvas> primitive because Canvas2D rendering, hit-testing, and dagre integration each surface unforeseen edge cases (text metrics, retina scaling, dagre worker message bottlenecks, drag inertia, focus management, keyboard navigation).
risks.mitigation: Build the primitive against the 3,000-node stress harness from day one — performance is a CI gate, not a final polish step. Cap Phase 1 features to: rectangles, parent-child orthogonal edges, drag-drop with cycle hooks, minimap, fitView, debounced position commits, pluggable node renderer. Defer to Phase 2: bezier edges, multi-select, keyboard shortcuts beyond fit/zoom, edge labels, custom edge styles. Reuse battle-tested libraries for the hard maths: dagre (MIT) for layout, d3-zoom (BSD) for pan/zoom transforms — we only own the renderer, hit-test, and interaction layer. If Phase 1 slips beyond 6 weeks, fall back to Cytoscape.js (MIT, canvas-rendered, scales to 10k+ nodes) as a stopgap behind the same facade so the Samantha API contract is unchanged.

risks.risk: Naming controversy (Rally 'Project' failure) repeats in Vector — Barclays-style 20-minute training arguments about what to call the page or the nodes.
risks.mitigation: Page name is 'Org Design' (descriptive, hard to misread). Node noun is gadmin-configurable per tenant via label_override — no imposed taxonomy. We don't pick a wrong word; we let users pick their own.

risks.risk: Federated handoff creates governance gaps: a delegated admin grants access they shouldn't, or builds a malformed subtree. No central oversight.
risks.mitigation: can_redelegate is a per-grant flag, default false (delegation does not propagate by default). Every grant/revoke is audited. Gadmin retains override at any node. Regulated tenants can lock delegation at the subscription level via a future tenant flag.

risks.risk: Schema migration to add org_node_id FK to portfolio_items + user_stories under a NOT NULL constraint requires a per-subscription root-node fallback to avoid orphan rows.
risks.mitigation: Migration is two-phase: (1) add column nullable + create root org_node per subscription + backfill; (2) flip column to NOT NULL after backfill verified. Same pattern as PLA-0005 addressables migration.

risks.risk: Clamp predicate on every list endpoint is invasive — feature teams may forget to apply it, or apply it inconsistently, opening data-leak paths.
risks.mitigation: Implement as middleware, not as a per-endpoint call. Add a lint rule that flags any list endpoint touching portfolio_items / user_stories without the middleware. Penetration test in acceptance criteria #6.

risks.risk: Drag-to-reparent has unintended consequences (rollups shift, dashboards break, access changes) and users do it accidentally.
risks.mitigation: Cross-team move preview modal (acceptance #7) shows the diff before commit. Drag without preview only works for sibling reorder within the same parent. Cross-parent drags always show the modal. Undo action available for 30s after commit.

risks.risk: Samantha API exposure of <DiagramCanvas> creates a stable contract obligation — changes to the primitive's prop shape or event surface break custom apps.
risks.mitigation: Version the Samantha primitive surface (samantha.diagram.canvas v1) from day one; semver internal API; document deprecation policy in the SDK reference. Ship the reference example app as a contract test — if the example app breaks in CI, the contract changed and we either revert or bump the version.

risks.risk: MVP scope creep — once stakeholders see the canvas, requests for re-delegation, multi-admin, archive-cascade, and matrix orgs land mid-implementation, blowing the 'functional MVP' framing.
risks.mitigation: Phase X deferrals are written into the plan, the leaf doc, and acceptance criterion #11. Story titles for MVP work include the suffix '(MVP)' so triage sees the cut. Phase X work gets its own future plan (PLA-NNNN); requests get parked there, not folded into PLA-0006.

references.label: R028 — Aggregator paper §8 (Path C clarified, C.2)
R029 — Canvas library evaluation (build-vs-buy evidence base)
PLA-0005 — Universal addressable element registry (substrate this plan adopts)
Cytoscape.js (MIT) — documented stopgap if <DiagramCanvas> Phase 1 slips beyond 6 weeks
